commit.message:
fix(stealth): eliminate Worker UA leak via process-level --user-agent

Web Workers have their own navigator object that CDP's
Emulation.setUserAgentOverride cannot reach. In headless mode, Workers
were exposing "HeadlessChrome" in navigator.userAgent — detectable by
CreepJS and similar fingerprinting tools.

Fix: when hidden=true (stealth mode), detect the real Chrome version
from the executable and pass --user-agent=<clean_ua> at the Chrome
process level. This overrides the UA for ALL contexts: main page,
iframes, Web Workers, and Service Workers.

Results on CreepJS:
  Before: 67% headless, Worker UA = HeadlessChrome/145
  After:  0% headless, Worker UA = Chrome/145.0.7632.160, 0% stealth

The stealth stack now covers 4 layers:
1. Chrome process: --user-agent=<clean> (Workers, all contexts)
2. Chrome process: --enable-automation removed (no infobar)
3. Chrome process: --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled
4. CDP injection: inject_persistent_stealth() (webdriver, plugins, etc.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix intermittent MSB3932 unzip error in dotnes.anese build (#166)

* Initial plan

* Fix MSB3932 unzip error by merging download and unzip targets

Merge _Download and _Unzip into a single _DownloadAndUnzip target in both
dotnes.anese.csproj and dotnes.mesen.csproj. This prevents the Unzip task
from running when DownloadFile fails due to transient HTTP errors (MSBuild's
AfterTargets doesn't respect target failure).

Also add explicit DestinationFileName to DownloadFile calls to ensure correct
filenames regardless of HTTP redirects, and add Retries=3 for resilience
against transient network failures.

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Keep separate _Download/_Unzip targets; add Error checks and retries

Address review feedback:
- Restore separate _Download and _Unzip targets (don't merge them)
- Add DestinationFileName to DownloadFile for redirect safety
- Add Error tasks after downloads to verify files exist before _Unzip
- Add Retries and RetryDelayMilliseconds from Directory.Build.props
- Move DownloadFileRetries/DownloadFileRetryDelayMilliseconds to
  Directory.Build.props as shared properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add duopyramid polytopes: {6}+{6}, {8}+{8}, {10}+{10}, {12}+{12}

Dual of {2n}x{2n} duoprisms, with vertices on S3 as two regular
polygons in orthogonal planes. Rings traced via reflection method
(all lie on great circles). Hopf fibration bundles used for coloring.

Key analysis results documented in spec.md:
- Rank formula: m+n if m or n odd, m+n-1 if both even (proved)
- God's number: m+n if full rank, (m+n)/2 otherwise (verified by BFS)
- Move distribution follows binomial coefficients C(m+n, k)
- Hopf bundles: n+1 bundles with sizes (n, n, ..., n, 2)
- Reference tables for symmetric and asymmetric cases

Support Attention(24)-CUDA and disjoint from contrib op (#27542)

Fix #27485
Reference https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/pull/27486

## Summary

Implements CUDA kernel support for the **ONNX Attention op (opset 23 and
24)** using a thin-dispatcher architecture that directly calls Flash
Attention, Memory Efficient Attention (cutlass FMHA), and Unfused
(GEMM+softmax+GEMM) kernels — bypassing the contrib MHA/GQA dispatch
layers entirely. This covers both Multi-Head Attention (MHA) and
Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) variants, with support for internal KV
cache (past/present), external KV cache (`nonpad_kv_seqlen`), boolean
and float attention masks, combined `nonpad_kv_seqlen + attn_mask`
composition, and 3D (BSNH) / 4D (BNSH) input formats.

The kernel respects `ORT_DISABLE_FLASH_ATTENTION` and
`ORT_DISABLE_MEMORY_EFFICIENT_ATTENTION` environment variables,
consistent with contrib MHA/GQA behavior.

---

## Architecture: Thin-Dispatcher Design

The core kernel (`attention.cc`) implements a **3-path dispatch
cascade** with clear priority:

```
ComputeInternal
  │
  ├─ Early reject: softcap, softmax_precision (not supported by any CUDA kernel)
  │
  ├─ Flash Attention    (fp16/bf16, Ampere+, head_size==v_head_size, no output_qk)
  │   ├─ Path 1: nonpad_kv_seqlen (opset 24 external cache) → mha_fwd_kvcache
  │   ├─ Path 2: past_key + past_value (internal cache decode) → mha_fwd_kvcache
  │   │           Supports: bool mask → seqlens_k, no mask → fill past_seq_len
  │   └─ Path 3: no past, no mask (prompt) → mha_fwd
  │   Excluded: nonpad + mask combo (no bias param), float masks on prompt
  │
  ├─ Memory Efficient   (fp16/bf16, SM75+, no past_key, bias stride aligned)
  │   ├─ Path 1: nonpad_kv_seqlen (±mask) → custom_right_padding + additive bias
  │   ├─ Path 2: with mask (prompt) → standard MEA with additive bias
  │   └─ Path 3: no mask (prompt) → standard MEA
  │
  └─ Unfused Fallback   (all dtypes, all mask types, MHA only)
      ├─ nonpad_kv_seqlen (±mask) → attention_bias with additive composition
      └─ Bridges to contrib MHA's QkvToContext (GEMM+softmax+GEMM)
```

GQA head expansion is handled via `LaunchUngroup` in the MEA path. Flash
handles GQA natively via `kv_num_heads` parameter.

---

## What DOESN'T Work ❌

| Combination | Reason | TODO? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **GQA + fp32** (any) | `MEA LaunchUngroup` is fp16/bf16 only; unfused
rejects GQA | ✅ TODO added |
| **GQA + float mask + decode** | Flash rejects float masks; MEA rejects
decode; unfused rejects GQA | Known limitation |
| **Softcap on CUDA** | All 3 kernels reject | ✅ TODO added |
| **softmax_precision on CUDA** | All 3 kernels reject | ✅ TODO added |
| **output_qk** beyond `kNone`/`kQK` | Only unfused supports, and only
`kNone`/`kQK` | ✅ TODO added |

\* 3 kernels mean Flash/MEA/Unfused

### Additional Known Limitations

| Limitation | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **attn_mask shorter than total_seq** | Spec allows padding with -inf;
validation enforces exact match. Tracked separately. |
| **No past_present_share_buffer** | Decode always copies past→present
(memset + strided copy). Contrib GQA can share buffers for zero-copy. |
| **No XQA kernel** | Contrib GQA's specialized decode kernel requires
shared buffers. ONNX Attention's internal-cache decode is ~15-30% slower
for GQA workloads. This does not apply when using TensorScatter +
`nonpad_kv_seqlen` (opset 24), which avoids the copy overhead entirely.
|

---

## Bug Fixes

- **2D mask shape interpretation (MEA path):** Fixed incorrect
interpretation of 2D `attn_mask` as `[B, kv_seq]` — the ONNX spec
defines it as `[q_seq, total_seq]`. The old code could read
out-of-bounds when `batch_size > q_seq` (e.g., batched decode). Fix uses
MEA's native broadcast flags (`strideB=0, strideH=0`) instead of an
expansion kernel, which is both correct and more efficient.

- **4D BNSH present_key/value (prompt path):** Added D2D
`cudaMemcpyAsync` for the `!is_bsnh` case in both Flash and MEA prompt
paths. Previously, `present_key`/`present_value` outputs were silently
left uninitialized for 4D BNSH inputs. Decode path already worked via
`mha_fwd_kvcache`; unfused already worked via `QkvToContext`.

- **Cutlass FMHA bias alignment:** Added alignment checks for attention
bias strides in the FMHA launch template, mirroring checks in the kernel
to prevent runtime enforcement errors on misaligned strides.

- **SEGFAULT fix** (`group_query_attention_impl.cu`): Added missing
explicit template instantiations for `LaunchUngroup<__half>` and
`LaunchUngroup<__nv_bfloat16>`. The new `attention.cc` calls
`LaunchUngroup` cross-TU, but without explicit instantiations the symbol
was undefined at runtime → `dlopen` crash → all CUDA test binaries
failed.

- **`val >= 0` assert fix** (`attention_mask_impl.cu`): Fixed overly
strict `val > 0` asserts in Flash kernel and AttentionBias kernel —
`val=0` is valid for count semantics. Kept `val > 0` in GQA kernel with
comment explaining last-valid-index convention.

- **Negative seqlens_k from all-false mask** (`attention_mask_impl.cu`):
When mask is all-false (`seq_len=0`) and decode offset is negative,
`seq_len + seqlen_offset` produced hugely negative `seqlens_k` passed to
flash. Fixed with `max(0, seq_len + seqlen_offset)` clamp.

- **BFloat16 Flash gate fix:** Fixed `disable_flash_attention_`
constructor gate that used `!std::is_same<T, MLFloat16>::value`,
accidentally excluding BFloat16 from Flash. Changed to `sizeof(T) != 2`
to match contrib GQA/PackedMHA/PagedAttention pattern.

---

## Changes by Area

### Attention Operator and Kernel Registration
* Added support for the Attention operator in opset 24 for CUDA,
including kernel registration for `float`, `MLFloat16`, and `BFloat16`
types, and refactored opset 23 kernel registration to use versioned
kernel macros for clarity and future extensibility.

### Env Var and Kernel Options Support
* Added `disable_flash_attention_` and
`disable_memory_efficient_attention_` members read from
`AttentionKernelOptions` via `GetAttentionKernelOptions()`. The ONNX
Attention kernel now respects `ORT_DISABLE_FLASH_ATTENTION` and
`ORT_DISABLE_MEMORY_EFFICIENT_ATTENTION` environment variables, matching
the pattern used by all contrib CUDA attention ops.

### Tensor Transposition Utilities
* Replaced 9 copy-paste `if constexpr` type-switch blocks with 2
templated helpers (`TransposeBNSHtoBSNH<T>`, `TransposeBSNHtoBNSH<T>`)
for efficiently transposing tensors between BxNxSxH and BxSxNxH formats
for `float`, `half`, and `BFloat16` types. Net -117 lines.

### Attention Mask and Sequence Length Handling
* Enhanced the mask-to-sequence length conversion kernel to support both
GQA and Flash conventions with a configurable offset, improved handling
for all-false masks, and refactored the launch function to accept the
offset parameter.

### nonpad_kv_seqlen + attn_mask Composition
* MEA path: supports both via `has_custom_right_padding` (seqlens_k) +
additive attn_bias simultaneously.
* Unfused path: converts nonpad to attention_bias, then composes mask
additively via `LaunchAddBiasInPlace`. Supports 2D `[q, t]` and 4D `[B,
1, q, t]` mask shapes.
* Flash path: excluded (no bias parameter when seqlens_k is used).
Warning logged on fallback.

### Alignment Checks for Attention Bias
* Added stricter alignment checks for attention bias strides in the
CUTLASS FMHA launch template, mirroring checks in the kernel to prevent
runtime enforcement errors.

### Helper Functions and API Improvements
* Added a new helper function for ungrouping in group query attention
(`LaunchUngroup`) and improved the output shape computation for
attention to optionally skip nonpad data validation for GPU paths.
* Refactored the CUDA Attention kernel class to split logic into
dedicated methods for FlashAttention, MemoryEfficientAttention, and
UnfusedAttention, each with ASCII dispatch diagrams documenting
supported parameter combinations.

---

## Testing

**39 C++ tests** in `attention_op_test.cc` covering opset 23/24
correctness, nonpad+mask composition (bool and float), BFloat16 Flash
guard, all-false mask regression, batch≠q_seq broadcast guard, and
nonpad edge cases.

New test files under
`onnxruntime/test/python/transformers/test_onnx_attention/`:

- **`test_mha.py`** (14 test classes, 16 test functions) — MHA path:
flash, MEA, unfused (fp32), BF16, 3D/4D inputs, bool/float masks,
past/present KV cache, causal mode, broadcast masks. Unfused tests
explicitly disable flash+MEA via env vars.
- **`test_gqa.py`** (10 test classes, 13 test functions) — GQA path:
flash, flash BF16, MEA, 3D/4D inputs, head ratios (32:8, 8:2),
bool/float masks, past/present KV cache, 4D BNSH present_kv correctness,
nonpad_kv_seqlen (Flash + MEA + CPU).
- **`test_tensorscatter_attention.py`** (5 test classes) — External KV
cache with `nonpad_kv_seqlen` (opset 24), TensorScatter-based cache
management, fp16 + fp32, with-mask variants (bool + float).
- **`common.py`** — Shared test infrastructure: ONNX model builders,
reference implementations, config classes.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix OCR agent crash after read_binary_file / when sending to LLM (#141)

* Initial plan

* fix: OCR agent crash after readBinary / when sending base64 to LLM

Two root causes fixed:

1. examples/agents/ocr-agent.md — system prompt now correctly describes
   the automatic ContentFilePart workflow; removed the outdated instruction
   to manually embed base64 text in the LLM message. The previous prompt
   caused the LLM to expect raw base64 but receive a ContentFilePart,
   breaking the OCR workflow after read_binary_file.

2. src/tools/predefined.ts — added a 50 MB file-size guard in
   ReadBinaryFileTool before attempting base64 conversion. Encoding a very
   large file requires multiple in-memory copies (ArrayBuffer + binary
   string + base64 string + JSON request body), which could exhaust the
   Electron renderer heap and crash the app.

Tests: 5 new integration tests cover normal read, file-not-found, the
50 MB size limit (both over and just-under), and shouldRequireHITL.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: enforce platform-aware file size limits for mobile Capacitor compatibility

- Use Obsidian's Platform.isMobile to select a tighter 20 MB limit on
  Capacitor (iOS/Android) vs. 50 MB on Electron desktop. base64 conversion
  requires ~4x the file size in RAM; mobile devices have far smaller memory
  budgets and would OOM-crash on large files that desktop handles fine.
- btoa, chunked String.fromCharCode and ArrayBuffer are all standard Web
  APIs available in both Electron and Capacitor WebViews — no other
  compatibility issues found.
- Add Platform mock to tests/mocks/obsidian.ts.
- Add mobile-specific rejection and just-under-limit acceptance tests;
  introduce setPlatformMobile() helper for cleaner test code.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Evaluation] Recover partial red team results when Foundry execution raises (#45541)

* [Evaluation] Recover partial red team results when Foundry execution raises

When orchestrator.execute() raises (e.g., ConnectTimeout on 1 of 50
objectives), attempt to recover partial results from the orchestrator
before falling back to the empty-result error path.

Previously, any single objective failure caused the entire risk category's
results to be discarded (data_file set to empty string, 0 results returned).
Now, completed objectives are processed through the normal
FoundryResultProcessor pipeline and included in the final output.

The error is demoted from ERROR to WARNING when partial results are
available, since it is not a total failure. The original full-failure
path is preserved when get_attack_results() returns empty.

[Evaluation] Fix red team status tracking, cache key mismatch, and evaluation error handling (#45517)

* Fix red team status tracking, cache key mismatch, and evaluation error handling

Bug 1 - Status tracking: _determine_run_status now treats 'pending' and
'running' entries as 'failed' instead of 'in_progress'. By the time this
method runs the scan is finished, so leftover 'pending' entries (from
skipped risk categories or Foundry execution failures) indicate failure,
not ongoing work.

Bug 2 - Cache key mismatch: _execute_attacks_with_foundry now uses
get_attack_objective_from_risk_category() to build the cache lookup key,
matching the caching logic in _get_attack_objectives. Previously,
ungrounded_attributes objectives were cached under 'isa' but looked up
under 'ungrounded_attributes', causing them to be silently skipped.

Bug 3 - Evaluation error handling: RAIServiceScorer now detects when the
RAI evaluation service returns an error response (properties.outcome ==
'error', e.g. ServiceInvocationException) and raises RuntimeError. This
causes PyRIT to treat the score as UNDETERMINED instead of using the
erroneous passed=False to incorrectly mark the attack as successful,
which was inflating ASR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(stealth): remove --enable-automation flag in stealth mode

When hidden=true (stealth mode), skip --enable-automation from Chrome's
DEFAULT_ARGS. This flag was causing:
1. The "Chrome is being controlled by automated test software" infobar
2. navigator.webdriver = true at the browser process level

Now filtered out in BrowserConfig::launch() when stealth is active.
Combined with the CDP-level patches (inject_persistent_stealth), this
provides complete stealth coverage at both process and protocol level.

Verified in both headless and headed daemon modes:
- No automation infobar
- navigator.webdriver = false

commit.message:
Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs (#5)

* Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs

Run 2-step overfit experiment on Qwen3-4B with LCBv6 (4 train tasks,
10 eval tasks). Teacher KL decreased 27.5% across steps, confirming
distillation signal. Zero accuracy expected at this scale. Includes
cost estimate (~$0.03 API equivalent) and comparison with SDPO paper.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add token accounting and grade timing metrics

- env.py: Add grade_time_s metric to SDPOCodeEnv.step() to separate
  sandbox grading time from inference time in future runs
- train_on_policy.py: Log teacher_kl_prefill_tokens (tokens sent for
  teacher logprob forward passes), train_total_tokens, and
  train_action_tokens (masked tokens the optimizer processes)
- experiment_report.md: Fix cost estimate to use actual Tinker API
  pricing instead of Alibaba Cloud rates; add prefill/sample/train
  breakdown; note timing granularity limitations

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Re-run experiment with token/timing metrics, rewrite report

New metrics now visible in logs:
- grade_time_s: sandbox grading is only 1.5% of wall time
- teacher_kl_prefill_tokens: exact teacher logprob token counts
- train_total_tokens / train_action_tokens: optimizer token counts

Report rewritten with actual Tinker API pricing ($0.07/1M prefill,
$0.22/1M sample, $0.22/1M train). Total cost: ~2.4 cents.
Inference dominates at 90% of wall time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Use timed() context manager for grading timing in env.step()

Replace manual time.monotonic() bookkeeping with the timed() context
manager from tinker_cookbook. The metric key changes from "grade_time_s"
to "time/grade", consistent with all other timing metrics in wandb
(time/sample, time/train, etc.). Remove unused `import time`.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add run log for v3 experiment (with timed() grading)

Re-ran the tiny overfit experiment after switching to timed("grade", metrics).
Confirms time/grade metric flows correctly through wandb logging.
Results: 8.4 min wall-clock, ~2.6 cents, 0% accuracy (expected).

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add 10x extended SDPO experiment (9 steps, teacher KL ↓74%)

Ran a 20-step experiment (9 completed before timeout) with the same
config as the 2-step baseline. Key finding: teacher KL decreased
consistently from 0.019 to 0.005 over 9 steps, confirming SDPO
training signal works. Accuracy remains 0% because LCBv6 is too
hard for a 4B model with 4096 max tokens.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add raw per-run log files from 10x experiment

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update report with 7 repeated runs + 9-step extended data

Combined analysis of 7 independent 2-step runs (variance estimates)
and 1 extended 9-step run (learning curve). Teacher KL decreased in
6/6 repeated runs (mean -34%) and 74% over 9 steps. Zero accuracy
persists due to task difficulty, not training failure.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

Add 10 CUDA vs Triton side-by-side comparison scripts

Each script JIT-compiles a CUDA kernel and runs the equivalent Triton kernel
on the EXACT same input tensor, then verifies numerical match.

Basic (4):
  01 Vector Add — exact match (diff=0), 10M float32
  02 Reduction — sum 16M floats, rtol=1e-4
  03 MatMul — 1024x1024, atol=1e-2
  04 Histogram — 256 bins, exact integer match

Advanced (3):
  05 Coalescing — 240 GB/s coalesced vs 55 GB/s strided (both frameworks)
  06 Tensor Cores — WMMA vs tl.dot fp16 matmul, max diff 0.0005
  07 Softmax — row-wise online softmax, max diff 7.5e-9

Production (3):
  08 Fusion — scale+bias+ReLU fused, atol=1e-6
  09 Quantization — per-row INT8 quant/dequant, exact int8 match
  10 LayerNorm — (2048,768) vs torch F.layer_norm, atol=1e-5

Includes run_all.py to execute all comparisons and print summary table.

tapping the mic button because:

   were in the source Info.plist but not merged into the built app plist

2. Missing com.apple.security.personal-information.speech-recognition
   sandbox entitlement for speech recognition TCC access.

   before starting AVAudioEngine. Added AVCaptureDevice.RequestAccess
